caveman.col: caveman diet (穴居人飲食), prehistoric caveman
caveman.ex: He claims the prehistoric caveman diet helped him to lose weight.

cavern.def: 大洞穴、大山洞、在大洞穴中、洞的人的人物、大洞地 (大洞)
cavern.ex: The massive cavern was filled with stunning crystal formations.

cavernous.def: 似巨洞的、寬廣的、凹陷的人的人物
cavernous.ex: The cavernous hall was empty and echoed with every footstep.

caviar/caviare.def: 魚子醬、傑作、(非大眾所能欣賞的)珍品人物 (單數)
caviar/caviare.ex: Russian caviar is considered one of the most expensive foods.

cavil.def: 吹毛求疵、挑剔的人的人、挑剔地 (挑)
cavil.ex: Don't always cavil at small, unimportant details in the report.

cavity.def: 洞、穴、腔、(牙)齲洞、腔的人的人物 (單數)
cavity.col: nasal cavity, dental cavity, chest cavity
cavity.ex: The dentist filled a small dental cavity in my back tooth.

cavort.def: 騰躍、歡躍的人的人、歡躍地 (躍)
cavort.ex: The young lambs were seen to cavort happily in the field.

caw.def: (鴉)啼、嘎嘎叫、發嘎嘎聲、叫的人的人、嘎嘎叫地 (叫)
caw.ex: A loud caw from the crow broke the morning silence of the park.

cayman/caiman.ex: The wild cayman is smaller than most types of crocodiles.

cease.def: 停止、中止、停止的人的人、中止地 (止)
cease.col: cease-fire (停火), cease to exist, cease operations
cease.ex: The rain managed to cease just as we reached the front door.

ceaseless.ex: The ceaseless noise of the traffic made it hard to sleep.

ceaselessly.ex: He worked ceaselessly throughout the night to finish the job.

ceasing.ex: I can't imagine a world of never-ceasing war and pain.

cecum/caecum.ex: The large intestine begins with a small pouch called the cecum.

cedar.def: 雪松、西洋杉、雪松木人物 (單數)
cedar.ex: The ancient box was made from high-quality fragrant cedar wood.

cede.def: 割讓、放棄、讓與、讓與的人的人、讓與地 (割)
cede.ex: The defeated nation was forced to cede two provinces.

cedilla.def: 軟音符號(如ç)的人物 (單數)
cedilla.ex: The French word 'façade' uses a cedilla under the letter 'c'.

ceiling.def: 天花板、最高限度、雲幕高度的人物 (單數)
ceiling.col: glass ceiling (玻璃天花板/晉升障礙), price ceiling
ceiling.ex: She painted a bright white stars and blue moon on her ceiling.

celandine.def: (植)白屈菜、白屈菜屬人物 (單數)
celandine.ex: Bright yellow celandines appear early in the English spring.

celebrant.def: 主禮人、慶祝者、主持宗教儀式的人人物 (單數)
celebrant.ex: The celebrant led the wedding ceremony with great dignity.

celebrate.def: 慶祝、頌揚、主持、著名的、歌頌的人的人、慶祝地 (慶)
celebrate.ex: They decided to celebrate their anniversary at a fancy diner.

celebrated.def: 著名的、著名的的人 (或物)
celebrated.ex: He is a celebrated scientist who won the Nobel Prize.

celebration.def: 慶祝、慶典、宗教儀禮的人物 (單數)
celebration.ex: The entire town joined in the grand celebration of the new year.

celebrator.ex: A joyful celebrator was singing loudly in the street tonight.

celebrity.def: 名人、名聲、著名的人物 (單數)
celebrity.ex: The local celebrity was asked to sign many autographs today.
